notes: Ubuntu is the most popular Linux distribution for desktops and cloud servers, developed by Canonical Ltd and based on Debian. Default/popular choice on AWS, Azure, GCP. Common base image for Docker containers. Ubuntu Core for IoT. Canonical is a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA). Downstream of Debian — many packages are shared. LTS releases every 2 years (e.g., 22.04, 24.04) with 5+ years of support. Ubuntu Pro (formerly Ubuntu Advantage) extends security with ESM (Extended Security Maintenance), kernel livepatch, and FIPS-certified packages. USN advisories cover multiple Ubuntu releases and often reference multiple CVEs.

data.description: Canonical's security advisories for Ubuntu. Each USN may address multiple CVEs and specifies which Ubuntu releases are affected.
data.notes: USN format: USN-NNNN-R where the suffix (-1, -2, etc.) indicates revisions or updates to the notice. USN advisories cover multiple Ubuntu releases and often reference multiple CVEs in a single notice. For bug tracking, see secid:advisory/ubuntu.com/launchpad. For CVE status across releases, see secid:advisory/ubuntu.com/cve-tracker.

examples.note: Suffix -2 indicates a revision or update to the original notice

data.description: Ubuntu's CVE tracking system showing fix status across all supported Ubuntu releases.
data.notes: Shows CVE status across all supported Ubuntu releases. Indicates which releases are affected and fix status. Links to related USN advisories. This is Ubuntu's own CVE analysis — for the canonical CVE record, see secid:advisory/mitre.org/cve. For official advisories, see secid:advisory/ubuntu.com/usn.

data.description: Ubuntu/Canonical's bug tracking system hosted at bugs.launchpad.net.
data.notes: Launchpad is used for Ubuntu and many other open source projects. Security bugs may be marked private until fixed. For official security notices, see secid:advisory/ubuntu.com/usn.
